chunk_summary: This section discusses CMAM program performance indicators and Sphere benchmarks for managing severe acute malnutrition, focusing on inpatient and outpatient quality metrics, recovery thresholds, and operational indicators to improve program outcomes.
learning_objectives: Understand Sphere benchmarks for outpatient therapeutic feeding programs, including recovery, death, and default rates.
Identify key inpatient and outpatient quality metrics for SAM management.
Learn to monitor operational indicators predicting individual treatment failure in CMAM programs.
Recognize the importance of protocol audits when performance thresholds are not met.
key_facts: Sphere standards for outpatient programs: recovery >75%, death <10%, default <15%.
Inpatient death thresholds are expected to be lower than outpatient benchmarks when stabilization is timely.
High-risk inpatient signals include deaths within 48–72 hours, often linked to shock, hypoglycemia, or fluid errors.
Operational indicators include failure to regain appetite by day 4, oedema persisting to day 10, and weight gain <5 g/kg/day after catch-up initiation.
Benchmarks must be setting-specific: inpatient and outpatient programs should not share a single target.
reference_quote: Sphere standards are commonly used for performance interpretation: recovered >75%, died <10%, and defaulted <15% among discharges.
